findings: Identity is correct: the illustrated adult male shows the diagnostic white throat and eyebrow bordered by black, chestnut/rufous mottled upperparts, scaled underparts, rounded body and very short tail seen in the supplied reference photos.

Anatomy count is correct: one head, one beak, two wings (one largely occluded), two legs, and one tail. Legs, feet, rounded wings, and short dark bill are plausible; the bill is slightly more hooked/prominent than in the photos.

All visible plate claims are supported: Colinus virginianus, Odontophoridae, male/female facial distinction, habitat, covey/ground-running/flush behavior, and the stated length and mass ranges. The listed 33–36 cm wingspan falls within the independently reported 33–38 cm range.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate.

verification_sources.title: Northern Bobwhite Identification — All About Birds
